description: Soft, flat cakes made from a batter and cooked on a griddle or pan. In many Western styles they are served stacked with butter, syrup, fruit, or other toppings.
core_ingredients: Baking powder or another leavening agent
preparation_steps: Mix the dry ingredients and wet ingredients separately, then combine into a batter.
Heat a lightly greased pan or griddle.
Pour portions of batter and cook until bubbles form and the underside is golden.
Flip and cook the other side.

nutrition.calories: Usually moderate; higher with syrup and rich toppings

health_advice.benefits: Can be satisfying and easy to pair with fruit, yogurt, or nuts for a more balanced meal.
health_advice.risks: Refined flour, syrup, and sweet toppings can make them high in sugar and low in fiber.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use whole-grain flour where possible and add fruit or a protein-rich side.

dietary_notes.note: Vegan and gluten-free versions are common when suitable substitutes are used.
